rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VARTKES ANIVIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/16/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please give 15 day's notice on DE-120 directly to ARF Fashnaksutyun Western U.S.A. and Aermenian Cultural Foundation not just their counsel. (Probate Code 1202). 2) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FERNANDO EXPOSITO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/16/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] The real property was appraised at $2,100,000, but the accounting begins with the reappraisal for sale value, not the date of death value. (If the value of an asset has decreased since the date of death, that FMV is shown on the separate schedule. [Probate Code Sect. 1063(a).]) The assets on hand also reflect 100 shares of SPDR S&P Transportation ETF, which was not on any I&A. Unidentified deposits totaling $16,510.18 (immediately preceding the executor's appointment as well as after his appointment) and multiple unidentified checks or debits after the executor's appointment totaling over $57,000 must be clarified. [Probate Code Sect. 1064(a)(2).] Who was the original payee of the three disbursements identified as "Bank of America Chk Acct #x1984" on 7/9/19, 7/12/19, and 8/12/19? [Probate Code Sect. 1062(b).] Costs paid to the attorney ($836.45) must be itemized. Under what authority did the executor pay legal fees to Utrecht & Lenvin LLP? Who is Sher Runge, and why were travel reimbursements made to him/her? The Probate Referee's commissions were reportedly paid in full, but the accounting does not reflect any payment to him. The decedent's will was dated July 18, 2017, not 2014. Is it Tara Walker, Tera Walker, or Teara Walker...? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/7/22. If the amended accounting petition cannot be filed in time to give 15 days' notice of it after it is filed, Counsel should contact the assigned Examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) Attorney requests $33,735 in extraordinary fees for services in connection with reassessment exclusion and estate tax return. Court to determine whether services in connection with reassessment exclusion was for the benefit of the estate (or solely for the distributee). Court to determine whether services in connection with the estate tax return ($3,300) is compensable, given that much of the ordinary services provided were in connection with the reassessment issue. 2) Attorney requests $50 reimbursement for "Hearing Fees" incurred on 5/13/21; however, there was no hearing on 5/13/21, and it is unclear what "Hearing Fees" are. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONY JER-FU LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, although no proper, file-stamped copy of the attorney's declaration filed on 1/10/22 has been received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. A will file has now been created with a certified copy of a will dated 11/4/98 and a certified copy of an Illinois order admitting a will numbered "18 w 3066". (When this petition was reviewed for the hearing on 11/24/21, neither of these had been lodged in a will file and only a photocopy of the one filed on 11/16/21 had been provided to this Examiner. The register of actions indicates that a copy of the will was filed on 11/16/21 and the original will [authenticated copy] was filed on 1/4/22.) The petition caption does not indicate that this is an ancillary estate, but if the will numbered "18 w 3066" referenced in the Illinois order is, in fact, this will dated 11/4/98, then this is an ancillary estate proceeding. Is an order acknowledging a settlement and appointing Jonathan Lee as Independent Administrator with Will Annexed but not identifying the will by its execution date, only by a number that does not appear to be on any of the copies submitted to this Court, sufficient to admit this will dated 11/4/98?

ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine whether to take the petition off calendar for failure to respond to the Examiner notes. 2) Examiner Notes: a. There is no publication on file for the amended petition. (See CRC 7.53 and Probate Code 8121.) b. Petitioner did not check a required box on the petition page 3 #5a. (7) or (8). c. Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] d. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SIGMUND A. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/16/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No proof of publication has been filed yet for the amended petition. [CRC 7.53(a).] No courtesy copy of the proof of service filed on 12/29/21 has been received yet by the assigned Examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Item 6 of the petition indicates that Decedent was survived by next of kin (box f), but if cousins survived, that is not the first box that applies. "Contingent beneficiary" is not a relationship to the decedent, nor is "cousin" without more. The "supplemental declaration" filed on 10/22/21 merely identified who the decedent's first cousins are, but did not set forth HOW they are the decedent's first cousins. Who were their parents and the parents' parents (the common grandparents), and are these the decedent's only cousins? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/7/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CYNTHIA COURTNEY REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST OF 2000, dated MAY 15, 2000
rulings.Calendar Matter: Verified Petition (1) To Recover Assets Of Trust And Estate. (2) Recovery Of Damages And Attrorneys Fees Under Probate Code Section 859. (3) For Breac
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the petition is ready to be heard. 1) No proof of service of notice of the supplement filed on 12/21/21 has been filed yet. [CRC 7.53(b).] 2) No copy of the trust has been filed yet. Petitioner says he does not have a copy of it. Respondent confirms that she is the successor trustee, but she has not filed a copy of the trust instrument under which she is reportedly acting. 3) Respondent also alleges that the real property was Cynthia Courtney's separate property before marriage and was still her separate property after the divorce, but she does not provide any support for this. 4) The date of Cynthia Courtney's death is different in paragraphs 2, 9, and 10 of the petition. While not critical to this petition, Petitioner might wish to clarify the date for the record. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OTIS R. DAMSLET
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/16/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proof of publication filed on 1/13/22 is an "amended notice" of a petition, not a NOTICE OF SECOND AMENDED PETITION, etc. The Court cannot admit two wills, even if they appear to have virtually the same provisions. Nor will the Court choose one or the other; the persons seeking to admit a will must proffer the will to be admitted and provide evidence for its validity. If the more recent will dated 1/10/18, only a photocopy of which has been lodged and is, therefore, presumed to be lost, is to be admitted, the petition must clearly indicate that the third amended petition seeks to admit a LOST WILL, and both mailed and published notice must clearly reflect that a LOST WILL has been submitted. [LRSF 14.20.] If the original holographic will dated 12/15/17 is to be admitted, a Proof of Holographic Instrument must be filed along with the third amended petition, to which only that holographic will, along with the accurate typed version of it, is attached as Attachment 3f(2). Please make sure to attach a complete copy of the will. (The copy attached to this "Petition Amended Second" is cut off at the bottom of the first page. The holographic will states in paragraph IV.D., "by the laws of California" without "the State of" and has a comma after "If he does not survive me" in paragraph IV.A.) If the petitioners are seeking appointment as co-executors (they have not yet been appointed as co-executors, so they should not be identified as such in pleadings) based on another document ("In Case of My Death") that original document must also be lodged in the will file. Evidence must be provided in support of the Court's admitting that document as part of the testator's will. (Ideally, other than the petitioners' own declarations without more.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/7/22 . Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SACHA HENCHMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/16/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) 1 proposed co-administrator is a nonresident of California. A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.[LRSF 14.18 E.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 1/31/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
